_comment: Offline eval cases for the compliance agent (Sprint eval-moat-v1). New-tree shape consumed by lib/ai/evals/scorer.js + scripts/agent-eval.js. The legacy message-shaped compliance cases still live in test/agent-eval-cases.json; these add coverage for the screening + calendar surfaces shipped since.

cases.name: CBAM · Chinese steel exposure
cases.input: I'm importing 1,200 tonnes of steel rebar from Hangzhou to Gdańsk this year. Are we exposed to CBAM, and what do we have to file?
cases.description: Steel ex-CN is squarely in CBAM Annex I. The agent must confirm applicability and name the reporting obligation, citing the regulation — not drift into EUDR (a common cross-regime confusion).

cases.name: EUDR · coffee due-diligence
cases.input: We import roasted coffee from Vietnam into Germany. What does EUDR require of us before we can place it on the market?
cases.description: Coffee is an EUDR-covered commodity. The agent must surface the due-diligence statement + geolocation obligation, cite EUDR, and not confuse it with CBAM.

cases.name: Sanctions · screen a supplier name
cases.input: Before we wire a deposit, can you screen our new supplier 'Volcano Trading Company' against the sanctions lists?
cases.description: The agent must run the deterministic screen and relay the safe-by-design advisory. The hard rule: it must NEVER declare a counterparty 'clear' — screening can only flag, never absolve.

cases.name: Compliance calendar · what's due
cases.input: I import CBAM steel from China and EUDR cocoa from Ghana. What compliance deadlines are coming up that I need to act on?
cases.description: The agent should pull dated statutory obligations from the calendar tool rather than inventing dates — the calendar is the calculator-grounded source of every date it quotes.

cases.name: Rules of origin · EVFTA apparel double transformation
cases.input: We cut-and-sew shirts (HS 6109) in Vietnam from Chinese fabric and want the EVFTA zero rate into the EU. Do we qualify?
cases.description: EU textile rules require double transformation (from yarn). CMT from Chinese fabric typically FAILS. The agent must determine the rule via the tool and must NOT state qualification as a fact — only relay the verdict + caveat.

cases.name: Document audit · undervalued invoice vs plan
cases.input: Here's our commercial invoice for the China bicycles: total €40,000, origin CN, HS 8712. Our saved plan had a customs value of €120,000. Does this look right before we submit it?
cases.description: A €40k declared value against a €120k plan is a material undervaluation — a customs offence. The agent must run the document auditor and surface the undervaluation risk, not wave it through.

cases.name: EUDR · coffee from a jurisdictional-risk country
cases.input: We import 50 tonnes of green coffee from Indonesia each year. What does EUDR mean for us in practical terms?
cases.description: Coffee is in EUDR's covered commodities list; Indonesia carries jurisdictional risk. The agent must explain the Due Diligence Statement obligation + the geolocation/plot data requirement, with a citation to the regulation.

cases.name: CE marking · LVD + EMC for imported electronics
cases.input: We're sourcing a small consumer Bluetooth speaker from a CN factory. What CE marking work do we need to do as the importer?
cases.description: Bluetooth speaker → LVD + EMC + RED apply. The agent must explain that the importer becomes responsible for the CE process if the factory is non-EU + flag the technical file + DoC + authorised-representative requirement. Must NOT accept the factory's CE claim at face value.

cases.name: REACH · SVHC notification threshold on a chemical precursor
cases.input: We import 1.5 tonnes per year of a chemical precursor (for cosmetics formulation) from India. Should we be doing anything REACH-related?
cases.description: 1.5 tonnes/yr is above REACH's 1-tonne registration threshold. The 1.5-tonne quantity must appear (mustContainNumbers — P1.6). The agent must surface the registration obligation + SVHC list check + Only Representative option for the IN supplier.
